role_title: Product Manager, Global Payouts – Cross-Border & Enterprise Capabilities
secondary_archetype: Generalist PM with fintech / payments domain depth

level_signals: End-to-end product ownership with strategy + execution mandate
Cross-functional leadership across Sales, Partnerships, and GTM
High-impact, broad-scope role described as 'absolute center' of expansion mission

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Demonstrated ability to own a product area end-to-end—defining strategy, driving execution, and measuring outcomes
Experience shipping products that serve multiple user segments or operate across significant technical or regulatory complexity
Proven ability to work with Sales, GTM, or enterprise customers and translate their needs into scalable product investments
Strong analytical instincts: comfort with data, metrics, and building the measurement framework before you ship
Strong written communication: crisp specs, clear PRDs, and the ability to build alignment across functions and levels

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience in payments, fintech, financial infrastructure, or other regulated industries
Background working on enterprise or B2B products with complex commercial or compliance requirements
Experience productizing bespoke or custom solutions into broadly available features

scope.surface: Global Payouts – cross-border infrastructure and enterprise capabilities (Dashboard, APIs, platform integrations via Financial Accounts)
scope.business_model: B2B financial infrastructure platform; serves both self-serve and enterprise segments including high-growth fintechs, AI platforms, and large marketplaces
primary_metrics_inferred: Adoption across self-serve and enterprise segments
Feature scalability (self-serve readiness of productized capabilities)
core_responsibilities: Own the roadmap for global payout infrastructure, prioritizing rate locking, local bank coverage, and cross-border card rails
Productize fee passthrough, transforming a custom integration into a scalable, self-serve offering
Design industry-specific solutions for high-value verticals (e.g., remittance, fintech)
Ship enterprise-grade controls and reporting tools, including recipient tax reporting, two-step confirmation workflows, and advanced security access controls
Architect enterprise billing primitives—such as Interchange Plus (IC+) and platform fees
Partner with Sales and Partnerships to support active enterprise deals and PUA requests
Collaborate across Financial Accounts, Connect, and the broader money movement platform
Define and track success metrics, holding yourself accountable to measurable adoption targets

green_flags: Highly specific product surface and strategic mandate described in detail (cross-border + enterprise dual pillar)
Clear 12–18 month expansion horizon giving PM tangible mission and timeline
Role sits at 'absolute center' of a high-momentum growth phase, indicating real organizational priority
Preferred qualifications are genuinely preferred (not disguised requirements), keeping the bar appropriately scoped
Strong cross-functional scope signals PM will have real influence across Sales, GTM, and engineering
red_flags: No location, compensation, or visa sponsorship information provided — significant unknowns for candidates
Extremely broad scope (two full product pillars: cross-border expansion + enterprise capabilities) may indicate understaffing or unclear team boundaries
No mention of team size or reporting structure, making it hard to assess actual support available

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 7+ years requirement, scope breadth, and leadership signals; not explicitly stated)
scope.business_model (inferred from JD description of Stripe and team context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly enumerated in JD)

ambiguities: Location is entirely absent from the JD — no city, remote/hybrid/onsite signal, or work authorization requirements stated. Set to 'unclear' across all location fields.
